title: The Fresno Nightcrawlers
author: Surveillance footage folklore
content: Mysterious, long-legged, pale entities captured on security cameras in California, resembling a pair of walking trousers.
action: Grainy, low-light footage invites the brain to construct uncanny patterns.

title: The Dover Demon
author: Local reports (Dover, MA)
content: A bizarre, thin creature with large, glowing eyes and a hairless body sighted by multiple teenagers in 1977.
action: Unidentified animal encounters are often elevated to supernatural status by collective fear.

title: The Bell Witch
author: Southern folklore
content: An invisible entity that allegedly haunted the Bell family in Tennessee during the early 19th century, physically tormenting them.
action: Unexplained household disturbances are easily attributed to unseen, malevolent forces.

title: The Flatwoods Monster
author: Local reports (Flatwoods, WV)
content: An extraterrestrial entity described as having a metallic, spade-shaped head, seen after a bright object crashed in 1952.
action: Sudden, shocking events trigger mass-produced narratives of alien visitors.

title: The Jackalope
author: American West humor
content: A mythical hybrid creature, described as a jackrabbit with antelope horns, popularized as taxidermy tourist novelties.
action: Folklore often blends natural anatomy with imaginative embellishment to create cultural icons.
